LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS WALTON LOGAN AND LEWIS DICKEY'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1048(B) IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING THE MOTION TO BE HEARD BY THE TRIAL JUDGE. BASED ON THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEIR PROPOSED BIFURCATION WOULD SERVE JUDICIAL ECONOMY. THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE BUY/SELL AGREEMENT APPEARS TO BE INTRICATELY TIED TO THE DETERMINATION OF THE MERITS OF THE DISSOLUTION CLAIM AND THE DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS IN THE EVENT THE CORPORATION IS DISSOLVED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PORTSMOUTH FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED. THE APPRAISAL PERFORMED BY FP TRANSITIONS DID NOT CONTRAVENE EITHER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT OR CORPORATIONS CODE 2000. THE CASES PLAINTIFFS RELY ON ONLY HOLD THAT THE PRICE-EARNINGS RATIO METHOD IS INAPPROPRIATE AS THE SOLE VALUATION METHOD. THAT DID NOT OCCUR HERE. NOR WAS IT IMPROPER TO CONSIDER THE UNEXERCISED OPTIONS AS OUTSTANDING SHARES IN DETERMINING THE VALUE OF THE COMPANY PER SECTION 2000. PORTSMOUTH'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Interpret Judgment And For Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, James A. Hughes of the law offices of Sullwold & Hughes (510-496-4616) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PORTSMOUTH FINANCIAL SERVICES' Motion To Interpret Judgment And For Order To Show Cause is GRANTED. Per it's inherent powers (CCP128(a)(8) and 187), the court clarifies that each plaintiff is entitled to a total of $121,140, each plaintiff has already received $50,000 that partially satisfies the judgment (Hughes Dec., pars. 4 & 11), and $71,140 remains due to each plaintiff. No later than September 29, 2017 plaintiffs are required to sign the Bill of Sale for Stock that is attached to the Hughes declaration at exhibit 5. The judgment orders plaintiffs to execute "reasonable documentation" to memorialize the sale and a bill of sale that warrants marketable title and includes an indemnity clause is reasonable. Portsmouth's request to set an order to show cause regarding contempt is denied as unnecessary at this point. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT PORTSMOUTH FINANCIAL SERVICES' Motion To Require Undertaking On Appeal Or In The Alternative For Order To Show Cause Re Enforcement Of Judgment is Off calendar at the request of the moving party. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Vacated

MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rod Divelbiss of JRA Law telephone number 415-788-4646 for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Walter Logan and Plaintiff Lewis Dickey.
James Hughes of Sullwold & Hughes telephone number 510-496-4616 for Defendant Portsmouth Financial Services.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling as to enforcement of the settlement and vacated the portion of the tentative ruling as to attorney's fees. Defendant Portsmouth Financial Services, Inc.'s motion to enforce the settlement agreement is granted. The appraisal performed by FP transitions did not contravene either the settlement agreement or Corporations Code 2000. The cases plaintiffs rely on only hold that the price-earnings ratio method is inappropriate as the sole valuation method. That did not occur here. Nor was it improper to consider the unexercised options as outstanding shares in determining the value of the company per section 2000. Portsmouth's request for attorney's fees is denied. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Hughes of Sullwood & Hughes telephone number 510-496-4616 for Defendant.
Rod Divelbiss of JRA Law Partners, LLP telephone number 415-4-788-4646 for Cross-Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS WALTON LOGAN and LEWIS DICKEY'S Motion To Bifurcate Pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure 1048(b) is denied without prejudice to re-filing the motion to be heard by the trial judge. Based on the record on this motion, plaintiffs have not shown that their proposed bifurcation would serve judicial economy. The enforceability of the buy/sell agreement appears to be intricately tied to the determination of the merits of the dissolution claim and the distribution of proceeds in the event the corporation is dissolved. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
